title: The Hidden Cost of Tying Self-Worth to Work
subtitle: 工作自尊的双刃剑

paragraphs.en: Many people associate their self-worth with their work. The more successful their career, the better they feel about themselves. Work-related self-esteem is therefore a worthy ideal to pursue with vigor, right? Well, not always. According to recent research, in which psychologists interviewed 370 full-time workers over a period of three weeks, the reality is a little more complicated. And it involves negative as well as positive consequences.
paragraphs.zh: 许多人将自我价值与工作挂钩。事业越成功，自我感觉就越好。因此，与工作相关的自尊是一个值得全力追求的理想，对吗？嗯，并非总是如此。根据近期一项心理学家在三周内采访370名全职员工的研究，现实要复杂一些，而且既涉及消极后果也涉及积极后果。

paragraphs.en: It's natural to be drawn towards pleasure and to step away from pain. In the workplace, if that pleasure comes from a triumph which swells our self-respect, people will try to repeat the accomplishment. But repeating that accomplishment is often not realistic, which can lead to severe negative emotional consequences when it doesn't reoccur. This form of motivation is widely regarded as a negative type of motivation.
paragraphs.zh: 趋乐避苦是人之天性。在职场中，若快乐源于提升自尊的胜利，人们便会试图重复这一成就。但重复这一成就往往不现实，当它未能重现时，就可能导致严重的负面情绪后果。这种动机形式被广泛视为一种消极动机。

paragraphs.en: It can hinder other more positive motivation types, such as completing a task purely because it's fulfilling or enjoyable. What consumes the employee instead is a pressing need to feel mighty and sure of themselves. They then take on only tasks and objectives which serve that ego-driven need. As a result, to avoid feelings of shame and worthlessness associated with failure, they extend themselves to such a degree that there's a subsequent adverse effect on their well-being.
paragraphs.zh: 它会阻碍其他更积极的动机类型，例如纯粹因充实或愉悦而完成任务。取而代之消耗员工的，是对感到强大和自信的迫切需求。于是他们只承担服务于这种自我驱动需求的任务和目标。结果，为了避免与失败相关的羞耻感和无价值感，他们将自己拉伸到对自身福祉产生不利影响的程度。

paragraphs.en: This internal pressure to succeed at all costs demands a lot of effort. It depletes their energy, culminating in disproportionate levels of damaging sentiment. Those negative emotions mount into heightened anxiety, impacting their ability to make the most of their personal life. Their desire to avoid feeling inferior ends up making them feel inferior when it comes to their diminished capacity for friendship and leisure. They end up dissatisfied both at work and outside of it.
paragraphs.zh: 这种不惜一切代价成功的内部压力需要付出巨大努力。它耗尽他们的精力，最终累积成不成比例的破坏性情绪。这些负面情绪升级为加剧的焦虑，影响他们充分享受个人生活的能力。他们避免自卑的愿望，最终因友谊和休闲能力下降反而让他们感到自卑。他们最终对工作和工作之外都感到不满。

words.meaning: v. 达到顶点，最终导致

paragraphs.en: But thankfully, for those people compelled almost entirely by this specific form of motivation, the news isn't all bad, or bad at all. The study also discovered several positive outcomes that can actually outweigh the harmful ones. Though these types of employees are motivated by the desire to avoid negative consequences, they are also motivated by the excitement of pursuing emotional rewards.
paragraphs.zh: 但值得庆幸的是，对于那些几乎完全被这种特定动机驱使的人而言，消息并不全是坏消息，甚至根本不算坏。该研究还发现了一些实际上可能超过有害后果的积极结果。尽管这类员工受避免负面后果的欲望驱动，他们也受追求情感回报的兴奋感所激励。

paragraphs.en: This excitement makes pursuing goals enjoyable and stimulates pleasure and pride that would result from success. An effect of the positive motivation is that it neutralizes the existence of negative motivation. Sure, it affects people's personal lives to what could be deemed an unhealthy extent, because leisure activities are often seen as a part of life that must be sacrificed to manage work and family demands. However, the way people feel about their work has less to do with whether they're motivated by the preservation of self-esteem but more with the fact that they're simply motivated.
paragraphs.zh: 这种兴奋感使追求目标变得愉快，并激发成功带来的快乐与自豪。积极动机的一个作用是它抵消了消极动机的存在。诚然，它对人们个人生活的影响可能达到了被认为不健康的程度，因为休闲活动常被视为必须为应对工作和家庭需求而牺牲的生活部分。然而，人们对工作的感受，与其说与他们是否出于维护自尊的动机有关，不如说更多地与他们单纯有动力这一事实有关。
